videos.title: GPT 5.4 hit a wall. It's over.
videos.summary: A hands-on comparison of OpenAI's GPT 5.4 against Anthropic's Opus 4.6 across multiple real-world tests: creative writing, technical explanation, geopolitical analysis, visual reasoning, and coding. The reviewer finds GPT 5.4 wins on benchmarks and visual reasoning but loses to Opus 4.6 on creative writing, instruction following, teaching/explanation quality, and response length calibration. Target audience: AI power users, developers choosing between frontier models. Biggest takeaways: (1) Benchmarks do not reflect real-world superiority. (2) GPT 5.4 has a persistent length-calibration problem. (3) For coding and visual reasoning GPT 5.4 is competitive, but for general AI agent workflows, Opus 4.6 remains the gold standard.

insights.title: Benchmarks are not real-world performance
insights.explanation: OpenAI publishes benchmarks showing GPT 5.4 crushing Opus 4.6, but in head-to-head practical tests, Opus wins 3 out of 4 categories.

insights.title: GPT 5.4's length-calibration problem is a design flaw
insights.explanation: GPT 5.4 consistently writes long responses when brevity is requested and short responses when detail is needed.

insights.title: Knowing when to be concise vs. verbose is a mark of intelligence
insights.explanation: Opus 4.6 naturally allocates fewer words where brevity was requested and more where depth was needed.

insights.title: 4-dimension model evaluation: Writing, Explanation, Analysis, Visual Reasoning
insights.explanation: Test frontier models across four real-world dimensions rather than trusting benchmarks.

insights.title: Do not use AI vision models for calorie tracking
insights.explanation: Both GPT 5.4 and Opus 4.6 significantly overestimated calories from a food image.

insights.title: Higher reasoning time does not guarantee better output
insights.explanation: GPT 5.4 spent 10x longer thinking yet produced a hedged, less decisive answer.

insights.title: Most people are underpowering AI with tasks that are too easy
insights.explanation: Current frontier models are far more capable than what most users ask of them.

insights.title: Match the model to the task category
insights.explanation: GPT 5.4 excels at coding, debugging, visual reasoning. Opus 4.6 excels at creative writing, teaching, instruction following.

content_ideas.title: Benchmark la gia -- thuc te moi la that
content_ideas.draft: GPT 5.4 vuot Opus 4.6 tren moi benchmark. Nhung khi test thuc te? Ket qua nguoc lai hoan toan.

content_ideas.title: GPT 5.4 vs Opus 4.6 -- Chon model nao cho viec gi?
content_ideas.draft: Bang so sanh thuc te GPT 5.4 vs Opus 4.6 theo tung loai cong viec.

content_ideas.title: Ban dang lang phi AI -- cho no viec kho hon di
content_ideas.draft: Canh bao nguoi dung AI dang mac ket o tu duy cu, chua khai thac het suc manh model moi.

videos.title: This is the easiest way to build AI Agents
videos.summary: Short sponsored advertisement for Airia, an AI agent-building platform. Describes the pain of building AI agents (jumping between 5+ tools, writing custom code) and positions Airia as a no-code/low-code/pro-code solution. Highlights built-in security, 3,000+ pre-built agents, and enterprise customers like BuzzFeed and Mars. Target audience: anyone interested in building AI agents without heavy coding.

insights.title: The real friction in building AI agents is tool fragmentation
insights.explanation: Building an AI agent today requires jumping between 5+ different tools, writing custom glue code, and hoping nothing breaks.

insights.title: No-code to pro-code spectrum wins the widest market
insights.explanation: Platforms that offer a plain-English no-code entry point, a low-code middle tier, and full pro-code access serve beginners and developers alike.

insights.title: Describe it in English, not code — the new agent-building model
insights.explanation: The mental model is shifting from write code to build agents to describe what you want in natural language.

insights.title: Don't bolt on security and compliance after the fact
insights.explanation: Many teams build AI agents first and worry about DLP policies, access controls, and audit trails later.

insights.title: The 3-layer AI agent platform stack: build, govern, deploy
insights.explanation: A complete agent platform needs three layers: a visual builder, governance controls, and a deployment/library layer.

content_ideas.title: Van de lon nhat khi build AI Agent
content_ideas.draft: Tool fragmentation problem -- 5+ tool de xay 1 agent. Goc nhin tong quat, khong de cap san pham cu the.

content_ideas.title: 3 lop can de xay AI Agent that su
content_ideas.draft: Framework Build-Govern-Deploy cho AI Agent platform.

content_ideas.title: Tai sao ban chua build duoc AI Agent?
content_ideas.draft: Video ngan 30-60 giay giai thich van de tool fragmentation va giai phap 3 lop.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Cowork Better Than 99% of People
videos.channel: Brock Mesarich | AI for Non Techies
videos.summary: Tutorial walking through five levels of mastering Claude Co-work: Import (migrating memory from ChatGPT), Foundation (setting up context files like goals.md and company.md), Workflows (using pre-built and custom plugins), Ecosystem (connecting external apps via connectors and Zapier MCP), and Automation (scheduling recurring tasks). Aimed at non-technical founders and business owners who want to use Claude as a virtual employee. Biggest takeaways: (1) Claude Co-work is a self-learning system that rewrites its own instructions. (2) The foundation layer is the critical differentiator. (3) Scheduled automations turn Claude from reactive tool into proactive employee.

insights.title: The 5-Level Claude Co-work Mastery Ladder
insights.explanation: Import, Foundation, Workflows, Ecosystem, and Automation. Each level builds on the previous one.

insights.title: Treat AI setup like onboarding a new employee, not configuring a tool
insights.explanation: Give it company context, goals, terminology, and SOPs. The more context upfront, the less you repeat yourself.

insights.title: The Claude.md Kitchen Bible Pattern
insights.explanation: claude.md acts as a self-updating instruction manual. After every session, the system updates its own instructions.

insights.title: Context files eliminate repetitive instructions
insights.explanation: By writing goals.md, company.md, and glossary.md once, Claude reads them at the start of every task automatically.

insights.title: Skipping the Foundation layer produces generic chatbot output
insights.explanation: Most people jump straight into prompting without setting up context files.

insights.title: One-prompt memory migration from ChatGPT to Claude
insights.explanation: Use Anthropic's official import prompt to transfer years of accumulated context in under 60 seconds.

insights.title: Why people don't switch to Claude: the cold-start problem
insights.explanation: The biggest barrier to adoption is not feature gaps but the perceived cost of starting over.

insights.title: Plugin-to-Schedule workflow promotion pattern
insights.explanation: Build a workflow interactively first, validate it works, then turn it into a scheduled task.

insights.title: Scheduled tasks require the computer awake and Claude desktop open
insights.explanation: Unlike cloud-based automations, Claude Co-work scheduled tasks run locally.

insights.title: One command should trigger an entire workflow, not a single action
insights.explanation: Design workflows as end-to-end pipelines, not isolated actions.

content_ideas.title: Ban dang o cap may khi dung AI?
content_ideas.draft: 5 cap do su dung AI: Chat > Foundation > Workflows > Ecosystem > Automation.

content_ideas.title: Sai lam lon nhat khi dung Claude (hoac bat ky AI nao)
content_ideas.draft: Paradigm shift -- onboard AI nhu nhan vien, khong phai cau hinh tool.

content_ideas.title: Chuyen nha tu ChatGPT sang Claude trong 60 giay
content_ideas.draft: Step-by-step tutorial chuyen memory tu ChatGPT sang Claude.

content_ideas.title: 3 file can tao TRUOC KHI dung AI
content_ideas.draft: goals.md, company.md, glossary.md -- Foundation layer cho AI.

content_ideas.title: Moi sang minh nhan duoc bao cao nay tu AI
content_ideas.draft: Show dashboard tu dong -- competitor research, trending topics, content gaps.

videos.title: Is Software Engineering Finally Dead?!?
videos.summary: Cole Medin shares his personal journey from refusing AI coding tools in 2024 to adopting them and now teaching thousands of individuals and teams. He introduces his PIV loop (Plan, Implement, Validate) system where he delegates almost all coding to AI assistants while staying in the driver's seat for planning and validation. The video addresses whether software engineering is dead and concludes that the role is evolving, not disappearing.

insights.title: The PIV Loop: Plan, Implement, Validate
insights.explanation: Sandwich AI implementation between human planning and validation. You plan what needs to be built, let AI do the coding, then validate the output.

insights.title: Software engineering is evolving, not dying
insights.explanation: The role shifts from writing code manually to orchestrating AI coding assistants.

insights.title: Don't resist AI coding tools — you'll fall behind
insights.explanation: Cole resisted AI coding assistants until November 2024 and regrets not adopting sooner.

content_ideas.title: Minh da mat 1 nam vi tu choi dung AI. Day la bai hoc dat gia.
content_ideas.draft: Cau chuyen ca nhan, co xung dot, de dong cam. Audience cung dang phan van co nen dung AI hay khong.

content_ideas.title: 3 buoc de AI code thay ban -- PIV Loop Framework
content_ideas.draft: Framework ngan gon, de trinh bay trong 60 giay. Plan > Implement > Validate.

content_ideas.title: Lap trinh vien khong chet. Ho dang tien hoa.
content_ideas.draft: Myth busting, gay tranh luan, tang comment va share.
